# UC-23.1.6 · Mobile App Crash Rate and User Impact

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Intermediate &middot; **Pillar:** Platform &middot; **Type:** Performance, Business &middot; **Status:** Verified

*When pocket software freezes or shuts itself down we tally how widespread that trouble is—not just totals—so fixes chase flaws that genuinely spoil everyday trips instead of reacting to meaningless spikes.*

---

## Description

Correlates handset-native crash envelopes from Crashlytics (SHA-256 fingerprint over canonical stacks plus variant identifiers), Sentry (`event_id`, envelope exception frames, Hermes/JSC/V8 differentiated stacks through release tags), Bugsnag (`errorClass`, `severity`, session identifiers), Embrace, App Center Diagnostics, and Instabug with Splunk-ingested session denominators so crash-free-session percentage and crash-free-user percentage reflect production cohorts—not desktop web vitals (that remains UC-23.1.3). The detection stitches fatal exceptions, Application Not Responding traces (`anrType`, main-thread stacks), watchdog termination signatures (`0x8badf00d`), iOS jetsam memory-pressure exits that rarely emit crash objects, Android tombstone-derived fatal signals (`SIGSEGV`, `SIGABRT`), React Native Hermes versus JavaScriptCore versus Android WebView stack shapes, Flutter/iOS Swift/Android Kotlin stacks after dSYM or ProGuard/R8 mapping alignment, plus optional `.ips` JSON v3 binary-image UUID hints when mirrored from Apple tooling. Leadership sees hourly crash budgets versus adoption-weighted sessions per operating-system major version and handset brand slices.

## Value

Product plus finance peers anchor roadmap dollars on crash-attributed churn bands rather than vanity defect counts—hourly crash-free-user percentages tied to anonymised subscriber tiers sourced from billing lookups expose recurring-risk exposure across Retail loyalty wallets, Financial Services authenticated sessions, Travel boarding-pass flows, and Media playback surfaces where interruptions translate directly into refunds or churn calls. Aligning Splunk-ranked stack signatures with Play Vitals ANR rate or perceived ANR dashboards and Apple crash summaries prevents duplicate mobilisation when backend outages inflate uncaught timeout exceptions inside the handset shell.

## Implementation

Deploy Crashlytics SDK plus nightly BigQuery export or scheduled Cloud Function forwarding JSON lines into Splunk HEC; initialise `@sentry/react-native`, `@sentry/cocoa`, or `@sentry/android` with outbound Issues webhook signatures validated via `X-Sentry-Hook-Signature`; configure Bugsnag Data Access pipeline deliveries posting JSON with `x-bugsnag-payload-version: 5`; route Embrace + Instabug HTTPS callbacks through Heavy Forwarder listeners; ingest parallel `session:mobile` heartbeat rows sharing salted `user_id_hash`; upload iOS dSYM and Android mapping.txt inside CI before tagging releases so Splunk-normalised signatures align with Firebase console buckets.

## Detailed Implementation

### Prerequisites
- Heavy Forwarder cluster exposing HTTPS collectors plus Splunk Cloud HEC tokens stamped with indexes `mobile_intel`, optional `business_lookup`, and TLS mutual-auth where finance mandates gateway pinning.
- CI pipelines uploading iOS dSYM archives and Android ProGuard/R8 `mapping.txt` per build flavor before tagging Firebase Crashlytics or Sentry releases—checksum manifests logged beside Splunk sourcetypes so mismatched symbols surface quickly.
- Python or Node workers authorised for Crashlytics BigQuery exports using Firebase service-account JWT bearer flows (`oauth2.googleapis.com/token`) feeding gzip JSONL batches into interim buckets replayed through modular inputs.
- Secrets vault storing Crashlytics tokens, Bugsnag auth headers, Sentry signing secrets outside repositories.
- Governance acceptance that mobile telemetry excludes regulated identifiers unless Legal signs salted hash bridges tied to CRM extracts.

### Step 1 — Configure ingestion
Segment vendors before Splunk touches payloads: initialise Crashlytics Gradle/CocoaPods hooks plus Gradle mapping uploads for each variant; initialise `@sentry/react-native` alongside native `@sentry/android`/`@sentry/cocoa`, enabling Crash Navigator breadcrumbs plus Release Health sessions while honouring sampling directives when budgets tighten; configure Bugsnag `notifyReleaseStages` so staging floods stay out of crash totals; configure Embrace/AppCenter/Instabug SDK toggles mirroring identical release identifiers (`CFBundleShortVersionString`, Android `versionName`, semantic versioning strings shared across Flutter/React Native wrappers).
Automate symbol uploads via Fastlane `upload_symbols_to_crashlytics`, Gradle `firebaseCrashlyticsUploadSymbolsRelease`, Xcode Archive post-actions feeding dSYM UUID verification scripts, Android `./gradlew uploadCrashlyticsSymbolFileRelease`, Sentry CLI `sentry-cli uploads`, Bugsnag Gradle plugin uploads mapping automatically—attach checksum logs into Splunk optional `ci:symbols` sourcetype for audit trails.
Webhook transports land on Heavy Forwarder modular inputs translating POST bodies into `_json` sourcetypes:
- Sentry Issues webhook hits `https://splunk-hec.example.com/services/collector/raw` with header `X-Sentry-Hook-Signature` containing hex HMAC-SHA256 digest—Splunk ingestion pipeline verifies signature against configured secret before indexing.
- Bugsnag deliveries send JSON lines declaring `x-bugsnag-payload-version: 5`; reject stale payload generations automatically.
- Crashlytics lacks durable tenant-bound webhook parity—mirror Firebase docs pattern using scheduled BigQuery SQL pushing hourly partitions through authenticated Cloud Functions emitting newline JSON into Splunk raw endpoint.
Representative modular input stanza (`inputs.conf`):
[splunk-crash-sentry-webhook]
connection_host = 0.0.0.0
handler = sentry_hmac_validator.py
route = /hooks/sentry
hec_token = ***REDACTED***
sourcetype = crash:sentry
Index-time parsing snippet (`props.conf`):
[crash:sentry]
CHARSET = UTF-8
KV_MODE = json
TIME_PREFIX = \{"timestamp"
TIME_FORMAT = %Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3N%z
MAX_TIMESTAMP_LOOKAHEAD = 48
LINE_BREAKER = ([\r\n]+)
SHOULD_LINEMERGE = false
TRUNCATE = 1048576
FIELDALIAS-app_version = release AS app_version_alias
SEARCH-time aliases (`props.conf`):
FIELDALIAS-os_alias = os.version AS os_version
FIELDALIAS-device_alias = device.model AS device_model
EVAL-crash_type = coalesce(exception.values{}.type,'unknown')
EVAL-user_id_hash = sha256(lower(trim(toString(user.id))))
Duplicate analogous blocks for `crash:bugsnag`, `crash:crashlytics`, `crash:embrace`, adjusting TIME_PREFIX patterns because Crashlytics exports sometimes omit timezone offsets—prefer `_time` sourced from ingestion receipt plus embedded event millis.
Route parallel BigQuery-export worker outputs through Cloud Scheduler hourly CRON invoking Functions POST with bearer tokens referencing Splunk HEC.
Document envelope-protocol nuances for Sentry (gzip envelopes permitted) versus newline-delimited Crashlytics exports.

### Step 2 — Build SPL plus summaries
Paste canonical SPL from `spl` field into macros `mobile_crash_core` plus companion macros summarising nightly aggregates via `collect` into `summary_mobile_crash` summary index keyed by `_time`, `stack_signature`, `app_version`. Maintain CSV lookup `user_arr.csv` exported from Snowflake/BigQuery blending anonymised subscriber tiers (`arr_band`, `avg_arr`, `churn_lift_pct`). Schedule hourly summary searches computing rolling thirty-day baselines feeding thresholds.
Persist caution around Spark joins—prefer `stats` dedupe followed by deterministic joins because duplicate webhook retries inflate counts unless dedup on `event_id` upstream.
Create alerts firing when crash-free-users metric dips more than half a percentage point versus prior GA candidate release (`previous_release_cr`). Store throttle keys on combination (`stack_signature`,`app_version`).

### Step 3 — Validation cadence
Hourly reconcile Splunk crash counts versus Crashlytics Issues dashboard totals filtered identical variant IDs and release filters.
Cross-check Bugsnag stability score dashboard versus Splunk severity-normalised totals (`severity="warning"` excluded).
Daily reconcile Play Vitals crashRate plus ANR rate exported CSV versus Splunk crash sourcetypes constrained identical package plus track (`production`).
Weekly reconcile Apple App Store Connect Crash Reports summaries versus mirrored `.ips` dataset counts.
Instrument synthetic crash via Firebase Test Lab plus Xcode Crash Reporter harness verifying Splunk ingestion latency median stays below fifteen minutes.
Document variance notebooks capturing Splunk webhook ingest skew versus vendor dashboards sourced from retailer geography splits.

### Step 4 — Operational response hooks
PagerDuty Events API v2 receives payloads annotating crash-free-users regression severity referencing Splunk dashboard URLs plus culprit stack_signature tokens.
Slack `#mobile-on-call` webhook mirrors PD summaries plus attaches Splunk-chart PNG renders without Grafana intermediaries.
Jira cloud webhook consumes Splunk alert payload templates issuing MOB-* tickets referencing impacted revenue_at_risk calculations plus owning squad tagged via lookup.
Maintain paging guardrail requiring concurrent Crashlytics velocity acknowledgement before declaring prod-wide Sev-1 unless Splunk correlation confirms regression spans majority geography shards.

### Step 5 — Troubleshooting modes
1. Beta/TestFlight plus Play internal-track cohorts inflate denominators—exclude rows lacking `environment="production"` and strip Play Console tracks labelled internal/open testing before trusting crash-free KPI math.
2. Forced-upgrade pushes pairing older OS tiers with freshly mandated compileSdk triggers ANR bursts unrelated to UX churn until phased rollout resumes across cohort windows.
3. Symbol uploads arriving hours late collapse disparate faults into `<unsymbolicated>` buckets—signature hashing merges unrelated crashes until CI uploads succeed.
4. Wrong Android mapping flavor referencing QA mapping.txt maps release stacks incorrectly—distinct crashes masquerade as single hashed signature.
5. JavaScript bundles shipped via CodePush/EAS Update mismatch native semver causing Hermes stacks referencing bundles absent from packaged binary—Splunk ties semantic versions incorrectly.
6. Hermes bytecode stacks diverge from JavaScriptCore stacks under identical JS faults—duplicate signatures fragment analytics unless release tags annotate runtime.
7. Watchdog termination codes (`0x8badf00d`) mimic deliberate force-quit absent auxiliary foreground timers distinguishing intentional exits.
8. iOS jetsam memory-pressure exits omit crash objects entirely—detect absence via Embrace foreground lifecycle gaps plus Android kernel events lacking tombstones.
9. Backend outage returning HTTP 503 cascades through Retrofit fatal wrappers inflating crash dashboards despite origin residing inside microservices owning latency SLO breaches.
10. Carrier-wide TLS brownouts spark OkHttp failures resembling handset regressions until Splunk overlays traceroute dashboards referencing ISP faults.
11. Third-party SDK bumps (Firebase major revisions conflicting React Native toolchain) introduce crash signatures untouched by product engineers yet dominate dashboards.
12. Velocity thresholds keyed on populations smaller than statistical significance spotlight ephemeral spikes irrelevant for roadmap committees.
13. Misordered SDK initialisation duplicates crash deliveries tied to differing session identifiers causing inflated dedup counts.
14. Anonymous identifiers resetting after reinstall inflate affected_users totals versus realistic humans.
15. ATT opt-out withholds advertiser identifiers limiting cohort joins referencing acquisition experiments.
16. Operating-system sunset tiers accumulate unavoidable faults faster than patched baseline cohorts—segment these tails separately before alerting executives.


## SPL

```spl
index=mobile_intel earliest=-168h latest=now
(sourcetype="crash:sentry" OR sourcetype="crash:crashlytics" OR sourcetype="crash:bugsnag" OR sourcetype="crash:embrace" OR sourcetype="crash:appcenter" OR sourcetype="crash:instabug")
| eval vendor=case(
    sourcetype="crash:sentry","sentry",
    sourcetype="crash:crashlytics","crashlytics",
    sourcetype="crash:bugsnag","bugsnag",
    sourcetype="crash:embrace","embrace",
    sourcetype="crash:appcenter","appcenter",
    sourcetype="crash:instabug","instabug",
    1=1,"unknown")
| eval app_version=coalesce(app_version,release,cf_bundle_short_version,m_app_ver,"unknown")
| eval os_version=coalesce(os_version,'os.version',device_os_version,"unknown")
| eval device_model=coalesce(device_model,'device.family',device_model_name,"unknown")
| eval build_number=coalesce(build_number,app_build_number,'android.versionCode',cf_bundle_version,"")
| eval environment=lower(trim(coalesce(environment,'deployment.environment','app.environment',"production")))
| eval crash_type=coalesce('exception.values{}.type',errorClass,issue_title,fatal_native_signal,"unknown")
| eval stack_concat=coalesce(mvjoin('exception.values{}.stacktrace.frames{}.function',"|"),mvjoin('stacktrace{}.method',"|"),mvjoin('stacktrace{}.symbol',"|"),stack_trace_plain,"")
| rex field=stack_concat mode=sed "s/0x[0-9a-fA-F]+/ADDR/g"
| rex field=stack_concat mode=sed "s/:([0-9]+)\\)/:(LINE)\\)/g"
| rex field=stack_concat mode=sed "s/kotlin.coroutines.jvm.internal.BaseContinuationImpl.*/kotlin.coroutines.generic/g"
| eval stack_signature=if(len(stack_concat)>12,substr(md5(lower(stack_concat)),1,16),substr(md5(lower(crash_type)),1,16))
| lookup user_arr.csv user_id_hash OUTPUT arr_band avg_arr churn_lift_pct
| eval avg_arr_fill=coalesce(tonumber(avg_arr),120)
| eval churn_lift_pct_fill=coalesce(tonumber(churn_lift_pct),0.008)
| eval anr_hint=if(match(lower(crash_type),"(?i)applicationnotresponding|input.dispatching timed out"),1,0)
| eval watchdog_hint=if(match(_raw,"0x8badf00d|watchdog"),1,0)
| eval oom_hint=if(match(lower(crash_type),"(?i)outofmemoryerror|jetsam") OR match(lower(_raw),"jetsam|memorystatus"),1,0)
| eval non_fatal_hint=if(match(lower(crash_type),"(?i)nonfatal|non_fatal|handled") OR severity=="info" OR crash_handled=="true",1,0)
| eval classification=case(anr_hint==1,"anr", watchdog_hint==1,"watchdog", oom_hint==1,"oom", non_fatal_hint==1,"non_fatal", 1=1,"fatal")
| eval crash_inc=if(classification=="fatal" OR classification=="anr" OR classification=="watchdog",1,0)
| bin _time span=1h
| stats sum(crash_inc) as crashes dc(session_id) as crash_sessions dc(user_id_hash) as impacted_users sum(anr_hint) as anr_events values(classification) as classes values(vendor) as vendors values(environment) as env_vals avg(avg_arr_fill) as avg_arr_lane avg(churn_lift_pct_fill) as churn_lane by _time app_version os_version device_model stack_signature vendor classification
| sort stack_signature _time
| streamstats current=f window=3 global=f sum(anr_events) as anr_roll3 by stack_signature
| join type=left max=0 app_version os_version [
    search index=mobile_intel sourcetype="session:mobile" earliest=-168h latest=now
    | eval app_version=coalesce(app_version,m_app_ver,"unknown")
    | eval os_version=coalesce(os_version,m_os_ver,"unknown")
    | stats dc(session_id) as sessions dc(user_id_hash) as total_users by app_version os_version ]
| fillnull value=0 sessions total_users
| eval session_crash_rate=if(sessions>0, round(100.0 * crashes / sessions, 4), null())
| eval affected_users=min(crash_sessions,total_users)
| eval crash_free_users_pct=if(total_users>0, round(100 * (1 - affected_users/total_users), 3), null())
| eval os_major=mvindex(split(os_version,"."),0)
| eval device_brand=case(match(lower(device_model),"(?i)samsung|galaxy"),"samsung", match(lower(device_model),"(?i)iphone|ipad"),"apple", match(lower(device_model),"(?i)pixel"),"google", 1=1,"other")
| stats sum(crashes) as crashes sum(sessions) as sessions max(crash_sessions) as crash_sessions max(impacted_users) as impacted_users sum(anr_events) as anr_events values(classes) as classes values(vendor) as vendors values(env_vals) as env_vals avg(session_crash_rate) as crash_rate avg(crash_free_users_pct) as crash_free_users_pct avg(avg_arr_lane) as avg_arr_lane avg(churn_lane) as churn_lane max(anr_roll3) as anr_roll_peak by app_version os_major device_brand os_version stack_signature
| sort stack_signature
| streamstats current=f window=1 last(crash_rate) as previous_release_cr by app_version
| eval churn_lift_pct_fill=coalesce(churn_lane,0.008)
| eval avg_arr_fill=coalesce(avg_arr_lane,120)
| eval revenue_at_risk=round(impacted_users * avg_arr_fill * churn_lift_pct_fill, 2)
| eval anr_rate=if(sessions>0, round(100.0 * anr_events / sessions, 4), null())
| eval fatal_present=if(isnotnull(mvfind(classes,"fatal")),1,0)
| where crash_free_users_pct < 99.5 OR fatal_present==1 OR anr_rate > 0.47 OR crash_rate > 2.5
| eval rank_score=sqrt(crashes)*impacted_users
| sort - rank_score
| table stack_signature app_version os_major device_brand os_version crashes sessions impacted_users crash_rate crash_free_users_pct anr_rate revenue_at_risk vendors classes env_vals previous_release_cr anr_roll_peak
| head 200
```

## Visualization

Dashboard Studio trellis of hourly crash_rate versus crash_free_users_pct faceted by os_major and device_brand; ranked `splunk.table` of stack_signature with revenue_at_risk and vendors columns; paired sparklines comparing Splunk crash_rate against Play Vitals crashRate ingest (`store:play_vitals` overlay); single-value headline from crash_free_users_pct min across fleet.

## Known False Positives

Internal dogfood plus TestFlight cohorts shipped with aggressive logging can dominate hourly denominators until `environment="production"` filters align with Play Console track selections—crash-free percentages look artificially bleak even though storefront shoppers remain steady. Crashlytics velocity alerts tuned for tiny handset cohorts fire whenever legacy Galaxy Note fleets spike even though statistically those populations cannot sway quarterly retention forecasts alone.
